zh.lifeSummary: 當Annabel de Stafford於1245年出生在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德時，她的父親Robert de Stafford29歲，母親Alice de Corbet20歲。她於1260年與Richard Hesketh在英國英格蘭藍卡爾可洛斯頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭藍卡爾可洛斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Annabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

variants.name: Annabell
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

variants.name: Annabella
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

variants.name: Annabelle
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Annabel de Stafford was born in 1245, in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, her father, Robert de Stafford, was 29 and her mother, Alice de Corbet, was 20. She married Richard Hesketh in 1260, in Croston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in Croston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Annabel de Stafford est née en 1245, à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Stafford, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Alice de Corbet, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Hesketh en 1260, à Croston, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée à Croston, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Annabel de Stafford 1245 in Stafford, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Stafford 29 und ihre Mutter Alice de Corbet 20. Sie hat 1260 in Croston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Richard Hesketh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in Croston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Annabel de Stafford è nata il nel 1245 nel Stafford, Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Robert de Stafford, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Alice de Corbet, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Richard Hesketh nel 1260, nel Croston, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel Croston, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Annabel de Staffordが1245年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードで生まれた時，父Robert de Staffordは29歳，母Alice de Corbetは20歳であった。1260年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアクロストンでRichard Heskethと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアクロストンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Annabel de Stafford(이)가 1245년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Stafford(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Corbet(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1260년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 크로스턴에서 Richard Hesketh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 크로스턴에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Annabel de Stafford родилась в 1245 г. в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Stafford было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Corbet, 20.
